heading: Vermont
label: 126 Vermont
text: Vermont constitutes one judicial district.Court shall be held at Bennington, Brattleboro, Burlington, Montpelier, Rutland, Saint Johnsbury, and Windsor.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesBased on title 28, U.S.C., 1940 ed., § 191 (, ; , ; , ).Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 11036 Stat. 1127Feb. 1, 1912, ch. 2637 Stat. 58Feb. 28, 1929, ch. 36045 Stat. 1345Provision that “any stated term may, when adjourned, be adjourned to meet at any of the other places at Montpelier or Newport,” was omitted as unnecessary and inconsistent with sections 140 and 141 of this title.Changes in arrangement and phraseology were made.
Amendments1984— provided for holding court at Bennington.Pub. L. 98–6201964— provided for holding court at Montpelier and Saint Johnsbury.Pub. L. 88–312

Effective Date of 1984 AmendmentAmendment by effective , and not to affect the composition, or preclude the service, of any grand or petit jury summoned, impaneled, or actually serving on that date, see , set out as a note under .Pub. L. 98–620section 411 of Pub. L. 98–620section 85 of this titleJan. 1, 1985
